title: SQL Filtering, Sorting & Limiting
description: Advanced filtering, sorting results, pagination, and SQL built-in functions.

questions.text: Which clause is used to sort the result set of a query?
What is the default sort direction when using ORDER BY?
Which query returns the 5 most expensive products?

options.text: SELECT TOP 5 * FROM Products ORDER BY Price DESC
SELECT * FROM Products ORDER BY Price DESC LIMIT 5
SELECT FIRST 5 * FROM Products ORDER BY Price DESC
Both a and b are correct depending on the database

questions.text: Which SQL standard syntax limits results to 10 rows using FETCH?
To skip the first 20 rows and return the next 10 for pagination, which clause is used alongside FETCH?

options.text: OFFSET 20 ROWS FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY

questions.text: Which logical operator returns TRUE only when BOTH conditions are true?
What does this query return? SELECT * FROM Employees WHERE Department = 'HR' OR Department = 'IT'

options.text: Employees in both HR and IT at the same time
Employees who are in HR or IT (or both)
Employees who are neither in HR nor IT
An error because OR cannot be used with string values

questions.text: In SQL operator precedence, which operator is evaluated FIRST?
Given: WHERE A = 1 OR B = 2 AND C = 3 — how is this evaluated due to precedence?
What does a CASE expression do in SQL?

options.text: Provides conditional logic, similar to IF-THEN-ELSE, in a query
Changes the data type of a column
Creates a new table based on conditions

questions.text: What is the output of the CASE expression below for a row where Score = 85? CASE WHEN Score >= 90 THEN 'A' WHEN Score >= 80 THEN 'B' ELSE 'C' END
What does COALESCE(NULL, NULL, 'Hello', 'World') return?
What is the difference between COALESCE and ISNULL?

options.text: They are identical in all databases
COALESCE accepts multiple arguments and is ANSI standard; ISNULL is SQL Server specific and takes exactly 2 arguments
ISNULL accepts multiple arguments; COALESCE only takes 2
COALESCE only works with numeric types

questions.text: What does NULLIF(5, 5) return?
What does NULLIF(10, 5) return?
Which function converts a string to all uppercase letters?

options.text: Both UPPER() and UCASE() are standard SQL

questions.text: Which function returns the number of characters in a string in SQL Server?
Which function removes leading and trailing spaces from a string?
Which SQL Server function returns the current date and time?
Which function extracts the year from a date in SQL Server? Example: DATEPART(year, '2026-02-26')

options.text: Both a and c work in SQL Server

questions.text: What does ORDER BY LastName ASC, FirstName DESC mean?

options.text: Sort by LastName ascending, then by FirstName descending within the same LastName
Sort randomly using LastName and FirstName

questions.text: Which query correctly implements page 3 of results with 10 rows per page in SQL Server?

options.text: SELECT * FROM Orders ORDER BY OrderID OFFSET 20 ROWS FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY

questions.text: What does NOT do when applied to a condition in a WHERE clause?

options.text: Inverts the boolean result of the condition
Removes the condition from the query

questions.text: What does LOWER('HELLO WORLD') return?
Which CASE syntax is used to compare a single expression against multiple values? Example: CASE Status WHEN 1 THEN 'Active' WHEN 0 THEN 'Inactive' END
What is returned by a CASE expression when no WHEN condition matches and there is no ELSE clause?
ISNULL(column, 'N/A') — what does this return when the column value is NULL?
Which of the following correctly uses TRIM to remove leading and trailing spaces?

options.text: Both a and b are valid in different databases

questions.text: What is the purpose of OFFSET in pagination queries?

options.text: Sets the maximum number of rows returned
Specifies how many rows to skip before starting to return rows
Defines a column offset for column selection
Shifts date values by a given number

questions.text: Which statement about ORDER BY is TRUE when used with OFFSET/FETCH?

options.text: ORDER BY is optional when using OFFSET/FETCH
ORDER BY must be present when using OFFSET/FETCH NEXT in SQL Server
ORDER BY cannot be used together with OFFSET
ORDER BY is only needed when FETCH is used without OFFSET
